book_claims.claim: One of the richest sources of dietary flavonoids important for cardiovascular disease and cancer prevention, especially gastric cancer
book_claims.mechanism: Contains fructooligosaccharides and thiosulfinates. Flavonoids prevent cardiovascular disease and cancer.

book_claims.claim: Italian sweet red onion Tropea contains taxifolin, a powerful inhibitor of many cancer cell types
book_claims.mechanism: Taxifolin directly inhibits cultured cancer cell growth. Red onions have highest flavonol levels; yellow onions contain half as much.

supplementary_research.finding: Meta-analysis of case-control studies found high allium vegetable consumption (including onion) associated with reduced risk of gastric cancer (OR 0.54). Confirms the book's cancer prevention claims.
Italian case-control study: moderate onion consumption (1-7 portions/week) significantly reduced risk of colorectal, laryngeal, and ovarian cancers. Dose-response relationship observed.

Ontario-grown red onions (especially Ruby Ring) were most effective at killing colon and breast cancer cells compared to other varieties. Quercetin and anthocyanins identified as key active compounds.

Quercetin, the primary flavonoid in onions, shows anti-inflammatory and blood-pressure-lowering effects in human trials. Bioavailability from onions is higher than from supplements due to the onion matrix.

culinary_pairings.source: Fellow alliums; foundational aromatic duo in virtually every cuisine on earth
Base for soffritto; fat enhances quercetin absorption
Onion-tomato base is fundamental in Italian sauces and Indian curries alike
Part of the Holy Trinity (Cajun) and standard Mediterranean roasted vegetables
Acid and sweet balance onion's pungency; classic agrodolce pairing
Classic French onion soup; thyme's earthiness deepens caramelized onion flavor
Onion is the base of virtually all dal and lentil soups; complementary nutrients

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Bioavailability from onions is ~50% higher than from quercetin supplements due to the food matrix. Red onions contain roughly double the flavonols of yellow onions.
Prebiotic fiber that selectively feeds beneficial Bifidobacteria and Lactobacilli in the gut. Not digested by human enzymes.

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: 25-50 mg in red varieties; absent in white/yellow
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Low absolute bioavailability (~2%) but gut microbial metabolites of anthocyanins are biologically active and may drive much of the benefit.
Responsible for the tear-inducing effect and antimicrobial activity. Destroyed by prolonged cooking.
